title: List
subtitle: A mutable, ordered collection of values indexed by integer position.

members.name: append
members.description: Appends the arguments to the end of the list. Returns self.

members.name: appendIfAbsent
members.description: Adds each value not already contained by the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: appendSeq
members.description: Add the items in the lists to the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: asEncodedList
members.description:
Returns a Sequence with an encoding of the list.
    Nil, Number and Symbol objects are copied into the encoding, for other
    object types, referenceIdForObject(item) will be called to request a
    reference id for the object.

    Also see: List fromEncodedList.

members.name: asJson
members.description: Returns JSON encoded representation of a List.

members.name: asMap
members.description:
The reverse of Map asList: converts a list of lists (key-value pairs) into
    a Map. The first item of each pair list must be a sequence. The second item
    is the value.

members.name: asMessage
members.description:
Converts each element in the list to unnamed messages with their cached result
    set to the value of the element (without activating).Returns an unnamed message
    whose arguments map 1:1 with the elements (after being converted to messages themselves).

members.name: at
members.description: Returns the value at index. Returns Nil if the index is out of bounds.

members.name: atInsert
members.description:
Inserts anObject at the index specified by index.
    Adds anObject if the index equals the current count of the receiver.
    Raises an exception if the index is out of bounds. Returns self.

members.name: atPut
members.description:
Replaces the existing value at index with anObject.
    Raises an exception if the index is out of bounds. Returns self.

members.name: average
members.description: Returns the average of the items.

members.name: capacity
members.description:
Returns the number of potential elements the receiver can hold before it
    needs to grow.

members.name: contains
members.description: Returns true if the receiver contains anObject, otherwise returns false.

members.name: containsAll
members.description: Returns true the target contains all of the items in the argument list.

members.name: containsAny
members.description: Returns true the target contains any of the items in the argument list.

members.name: containsIdenticalTo
members.description:
Returns true if the receiver contains a value identical to anObject,
    otherwise returns false.

members.name: copy
members.description: Replaces self with <tt>v</tt> list items. Returns self.

members.name: cursor
members.description: Returns a ListCursor for the receiver.

members.name: detect
members.description:
Returns the first value for which the message evaluates to a non-nil. Example: 
list(1, 2, 3, 4) detect(i, v, v > 2)
==> 3
list(1, 2, 3, 4) detect(v, v > 2)
==> 3

members.name: difference
members.description: Returns a new list containing items from the target list which aren't in the argument list.

members.name: empty
members.description: Removes all items from the receiver.

members.name: first
members.description:
Returns the first item or Nil if the list is empty.
    If optionalSize is provided, that number of the first items in the list are
    returned.

members.name: flatten
members.description: Creates a new list, with all contained lists flattened into the new list. For example: list(1,2,list(3,4,list(5))) flatten ==> list(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)

members.name: foreach
members.description:
Loops over the list values setting the specified index and value slots and executing the message. Returns the result of the last execution of the message. Example: 

list(1, 2, 3) foreach(i, v, writeln(i, " = ", v))
list(1, 2, 3) foreach(v, writeln(v))

members.name: fromEncodedList
members.description:
Returns a List with the decoded Nils, Symbols and Numbers from the input raw
    array. For each object reference encounters, objectForReferenceId(id) will
    be called to allow the reference to be resolved.

    Also see: List asEncodedList.

members.name: groupBy
indexOf
members.description:
Returns the index of the first occurrence of anObject
    in the receiver. Returns Nil if the receiver doesn't contain anObject.

members.name: insertAfter
members.description:
Inserts item after first occurrence of afterItem and returns self. If afterItem
    is not found, item is appended to the end of the list.

members.name: insertAt
members.description:
Inserts item at the specified index. Raises an exception if the index is
    out of bounds. Returns self.

members.name: insertBefore
members.description:
Inserts item before first occurrence of beforeItem or to the end of the list if
    beforeItem is not found. Returns self.

members.name: intersect
members.description: Returns a new list containing the common values from the target and argument lists.

members.name: itemCopy
members.description: Returns a new list containing the items from the receiver.

members.name: join
members.description:
Returns a Sequence of the concatenated items with
    optionalSeparator between each item or simply the concatenation of the items if no optionalSeparator is supplied.

members.name: justSerialized
members.description: Writes the receiver's code into the stream.

members.name: last
members.description:
Returns the last item or Nil if the list is empty.
    If optionalSize is provided, that number of the last items in the list are
    returned.

members.name: map
members.description: Same as <tt>mapInPlace</tt>, but returns results in a new List.

members.name: mapInPlace
members.description:
Replaces each item in the receiver with the result of applying a given message to that item. Example: 
list(1, 5, 7, 2) mapInPlace(i, v, i + v)
==> list(1, 6, 9, 5)
list(1, 5, 7, 2) mapInPlace(v, v + 3)
 ==> list(4, 8, 10, 5)

members.name: pop
members.description:
Returns the last item in the list and removes it
    from the receiver. Returns nil if the receiver is empty.

members.name: preallocateToSize
members.description: Preallocates array memory to hold aNumber number of items.

members.name: prepend
members.description: Inserts the values at the beginning of the list. Returns self.

members.name: push
members.description: Same as add(anObject1, anObject2, ...).

members.name: reduce
members.description:
Also known as foldl or inject. Combines values in target starting on the left. If no initial value is passed the head of the list is used. 
Io> list(1, 2, 3) reduce(+)
==> 6
Io> list(1, 2, 3) reduce(xs, x, xs + x)
==> 6
Io> list(1, 2, 3) reduce(+, -6) # Passing the initial value.
==> 0
Io> list(1, 2, 3) reduce(xs, x, xs + x, -6)
==> 0

members.name: remove
members.description: Removes all occurrences of the arguments from the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: removeAt
members.description:
Removes the item at the specified index and returns the value removed.
    Raises an exception if the index is out of bounds.

members.name: removeFirst
members.description: Returns the first item and removes it from the list. nil is returned if the list is empty.

members.name: removeLast
members.description: Returns the last item and removes it from the list. nil is returned if the list is empty.

members.name: removeSeq
members.description: Removes each of the items from the current list which are contained in the sequence passed in.

members.name: rest
members.description: Returns a copy of the list but with the first element removed.

members.name: reverse
members.description: Reverses the ordering of all the items of the receiver. Returns copy of receiver.

members.name: reverseForeach
members.description: Same as foreach, but in reverse order.

members.name: reverseInPlace
members.description: Reverses the ordering of all the items in the receiver. Returns self.

members.name: reverseReduce
members.description:
Also known as foldr or inject. Combines values in target starting on the right.
    If no initial value is paseed the head of the list is used. See List reverse for examples.

members.name: second
members.description: Returns third element (same as <tt>at(2)</tt>)

members.name: select
members.description: Same as <tt>selectInPlace</tt>, but result is a new List.

members.name: selectInPlace
members.description:
Like foreach, but the values for which the result of message is either nil or false are removed from the List. Example: 
list(1, 5, 7, 2) selectInPlace(i, v, v > 3)
==> 5, 7
list(1, 5, 7, 2) selectInPlace(v, v > 3)
 ==> 5, 7

members.name: setSize
members.description:
Sets the size of the receiver by either removing excess items or adding nils
    as needed.

members.name: size
members.description: Returns the number of items in the receiver.

members.name: slice
members.description:
Returns a new string containing the subset of the receiver
from the startIndex to the endIndex. The endIndex argument
    is optional. If not given, it is assumed to be the end of the string.
Step argument is also optional and defaults to 1, if not given.
However, since Io supports positional arguments only, you need to
explicitly specify endIndex, if you need a custom step.

members.name: sliceInPlace
members.description:
Returns the receiver containing the subset of the
    receiver from the startIndex to the endIndex. The endIndex argument
    is optional. If not given, it is assumed to be the end of the string.
Step argument is also optional and defaults to 1.

members.name: sort
members.description: Returns a new list containing the sorted items from the receiver.

members.name: sortBy
members.description: Returns a new list containing the items from the receiver, sorted using aBlock as compare function. Example: list(1, 3, 2, 4, 0) sortBy(block(a, b, a > b)) ==> list(4, 3, 2, 1, 0)

members.name: sortInPlace
members.description:
Sorts the list using the compare method on the items. Returns self.
    If an optionalExpression is provided, the sort is done on the result of the
    evaluation of the optionalExpression on each value.

members.name: sortInPlaceBy
members.description: Sort the list using aBlock as the compare function. Returns self.

members.name: sum
members.description: Returns the sum of the items.

members.name: swapIndices
members.description:
Exchanges the object at index1 with the object at index2.
    Raises an exception if either index is out of bounds. Returns self.

members.name: union
members.description: Returns a new list containing items from the target and items which are only in the argument list.

members.name: unique
members.description: Returns a new list containing all the values in the target, but no duplicates.

members.name: uniqueCount
members.description: Returns a list of list(value, count) for each unique value in self.

members.name: with
members.description: Returns a new List containing the arguments.
